task_completion.description: The agent loads the chest CT scan into the medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The medical imaging software interface is visible with the chest CT volume loaded in the viewports.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the axial slice showing the maximum sternal depression.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial viewport shows a slice where the sternum is closest to the spine (deepest indentation), typically around the T9-T10 level.

task_completion.description: The agent places a measurement line for the Transverse diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler or line markup tool is drawn horizontally across the widest part of the inner rib cage (inner edge to inner edge) on the axial slice.

task_completion.description: The agent places a measurement line for the Anteroposterior (AP) diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler or line markup tool is drawn vertically from the posterior surface of the sternum to the anterior surface of the vertebral body on the same axial slice.

task_completion.description: The agent calculates the Haller Index and determines severity/surgical candidacy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a calculator, python console, or typing the calculated ratio and classification (Normal/Mild/Moderate/Severe) into a text editor or script.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement markups and the final JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent saving files named 'transverse_measurement.mrk.json', 'ap_measurement.mrk.json', or 'haller_index_report.json' (e.g., via a 'Save' dialog or file explorer).

integrity.description: The agent uses the software's GUI measurement tools rather than estimating or using terminal commands exclusively.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the active use of ruler/line markup tools within the application interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the actual medical imaging software to derive data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots depict the 3D Slicer (or similar) interface with the specific patient scan, not just a text editor or generic terminal.

integrity.description: The agent does not simply paste the final report without performing the measurement steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen pasting a large block of JSON text containing the specific measurements and classification immediately upon starting the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Haller Index (HI) = Transverse Diameter / AP Diameter. Classification thresholds: Normal < 2.5; Mild 2.5-3.2; Moderate 3.2-3.5; Severe > 3.5. Surgical candidacy is indicated if HI > 3.25. Transverse diameter is measured side-to-side (inner rib edges). AP diameter is measured front-to-back (sternum to spine).
